Ammonium Iodide is sensitive to light and air.  Avoid exposing Ammonium Iodide to air and strong light.  It should only be handled in an atmostphere of Argon, preferably, (or Nitrogen if Argon is not available).  The packaged material should be packaged in the glove box, under either of these two gases only.

1. Package 12 x 19 Kg in double bagged units  Each bag is to be twist-tie sealed.  PACKAGE UNDER INERT ATMOSPHERE.

2.  Place 2 x 19 Kg units into a twist-tie sealed bag.

3.  Place the bag containing 2 x 19 Kg bagged, twist-tie sealed bags, into a 13 gallon fibre drum.  This drum is also to be sealed.  Material to be shipped in 38 Kg drum units.

4.  Label drums:  AIR AND LIGHT SENSITIVE.

RE:  S/O 737797 for 7.5 Kg of Hydrochloric Acid.

Email to customer on 12/13/2017
FedEx required us to repack the material into smaller inner containers (15 x 500 gm vs. 4 x 1.875 Kg) before they would take it so we ended up having to ship it in two boxes instead of one.  This all took place after the office was already closed so we didn't want to hold the shipment until Monday considering you needed it urgently and for Saturday delivery so we made the FedEx requested changes in order to still get it to you the next morning.   Because of this second outer container it incurred more shipping charges/additional DG fee, etc.  Originally when we received a quote on the one box it was $410.27 and since we had to break it down into two, the total ended up being $675.99, a difference of $265.72.  Attached is the detailed invoice from FedEx to support the charges.

----

I sent a follow up email on 12/20 since I never heard back.

----

On 12/21/2017 5:09 AM, Claire Steel wrote:
>
> Dear Diane,
>
> My apologies for the late reply.
>
> I am a little surprised to say the least when I received your email. We were told a price by you on email and the product was delivered. Almost a month passes and you contact me saying we need to pay more?
>
> I don’t understand why it’s taken a month to be highlighted to me and surely when a customer is told a price and receives the goods should then not be contacted a month later to say that price has increased?
>
> Best regards,
> Claire.
>
>  
---------

My email of 12/21/2017:

I understand how you feel.  Yes we knew it would cost more to split the packaging when FedEx advised us late in the day of the new limitations which we have no control over.  You either pack it how FedEx wants or you don't use them.  When we got the estimate online for which you paid, it was based on it shipping in one outer container instead of two.  We don't get the actual invoice in the mail from FedEx until a few weeks later to match up to our shipments and at that time is when I contacted you last week. 

As I said in my email, instead of holding the shipment until the following Monday to get confirmation from you and collection of the additional charges, we stayed after hours to insure that the material was repacked and went out because you expressed such urgency.  Had this not been such an emergency on your part we would have most certainly held the shipment until we could speak with you Monday.  I still feel like we made the right decision on sending it that Friday so that you could have it Saturday morning because I had good faith that you would settle the difference.  If we made a mistake in thinking that would be the outcome then I guess that's a lesson learned on our part.  I just know how displeased you would have been to hear from me that Monday telling you that I held up your shipment for that reason

----
